mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate alongside rising payroll employment signals strengthening labor market fundamentals that directly support corporate revenue growth and equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in declining unemployment periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid federal funds rate increases exceeding 25 basis points in a single month while core inflation remains elevated compress equity discount rates and trigger risk-off positioning, producing negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp in 1 month while CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% YoY
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the joint coefficient on rate hikes and sticky inflation becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous expansion of consumer credit balances and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and wealth effects that sustain corporate earnings growth, driving positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show no positive correlation with the credit-housing composite index, or the interaction term coefficient flips sign during credit contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, accelerating industrial production growth directly translates to near-term equity outperformance as capacity utilization peaks and profit margins expand before demand decelerates.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns when the ISM Manufacturing PMI falls below 50, or the rolling 1-year correlation coefficient drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth momentum positively correlates with forward equity returns over a quarter as earnings revisions align with macro fundamentals and institutional capital flows follow growth trajectories.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in positive GDP growth quarters are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, tight labor market conditions drive wage growth and consumer spending that directly boost corporate revenues, producing positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 4.0%, or the forward return differential between sub-4.0% and above-4.0% periods is statistically indistinguishable (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The real interest rate, calculated as the federal funds rate minus trailing CPI inflation, acts as a primary discount rate lever; when the real rate rises, equity valuations contract immediately due to higher required returns on future cash flows.
failure_condition: Equity returns show no negative sensitivity to rising real rates, or the interaction coefficient becomes positive during disinflationary periods, invalidating the discount rate channel.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery regime, the joint acceleration of consumer credit and payroll employment reliably predicts positive equity returns as liquidity constraints ease and income growth resumes, supporting near-term market performance.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are flat or negative when both credit and employment accelerate simultaneously, or the joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy over a 5-year walk-forward test.
